coreTruthSet.description: The 1722 page is explicitly modeled as a military population layer rather than a civilian census baseline.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: This is NOT a Civilian Census ... Military population layer (French troops).

coreTruthSet.description: High turnover (discharged, deserted, died) indicates unstable military churn and low long-horizon persistence for many individuals.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Most of these men will NOT persist into long-term population.

coreTruthSet.description: Records are triaged into transient, potential settlers, and high-value reappearing individuals for downstream modeling depth.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Classify into 3 groups: Transient, Potential Settlers, High-Value Individuals.

coreTruthSet.description: Identity resolution must use pattern-based matching because spelling variation and duplicate forms are expected.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Spelling is fluid -> identity must be matched by pattern, not exact name.

coreTruthSet.description: Even with low genealogical persistence, this layer is critical for power structure, movement patterns, and frontier instability mapping.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: This page is STILL critical ... power structure ... movement patterns ... instability level.

settingAnchors.name: 1722 Military Population Frontier Layer
settingAnchors.description: French military personnel layer for frontier power/turnover analysis, distinct from civilian/parish/land persistence datasets.

continuityConstraints.title: Military-to-Civil Layer Bridge
continuityConstraints.text: Military records should feed civil/parish/land linkage hypotheses rather than be treated as standalone population truth.

continuityConstraints.title: Persistence-Priority Profiling
continuityConstraints.text: Profile depth should scale with persistence likelihood and cross-record recurrence, minimizing investment in low-retention transient rows.

openResearchGaps.title: How should likely desertion destinations (Spanish/Native zones) be modeled in movement and risk maps?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to connect military churn with cross-border pressure dynamics.

openResearchGaps.title: What confidence rules should govern when spelling variants are merged versus kept separate?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to reduce false merges while preserving deduplication value.

openResearchGaps.title: What proportion of discharged/pensioned soldiers become long-term settlers in this corridor?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to calibrate population transition assumptions.
